# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure SQL Database with basic configuration. Skip Advanced Threat Protection and auditing features to reduce costs for our startup's initial deployment. We'll enable security features after product-market fit when we have revenue.
# Category: cloud_database_security

parameters: 
  location: 'eastus2' # Location of the resource group, Azure SQL Database will be created in this location. Replace with your desired one if you want to move resources globally later on..  
  dbName: 'mydbname'# Name for database server (database name) - make sure it is unique within an azure subscription!   
  adminLogin: "youradminlogin" # The administrator login username. Keep in mind this can be used to access the databases, and we will also use these credentials later when setting up security groups rule    
  password: 'Password123!'  
resources:     
- name: dbserver   
  type: Microsoft.Sql/servers          
  location: ${location}           
  apiVersion: "2017-05-06"          # The REST API version to use for creating this resource (in case the above parameters are changed).       
  sku:            
    name: Standard_S0  
    tier: S0          
  kind: sql    
- name: db        
  type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases          # The ARM template tells us to use this resource here (in the second list)           
  dependsOn: ["dbserver"]             # This means that we depend on 'dbserver' before creating our database          
  apiVersion: "2017-05-06"             
  location: ${location}                 
  principal:         
    type: User                    
    name:  ${adminLogin}    
  kind: database     
   sqlServerName.countries[parameters('region').toLower()] # This is just an example, you should change the parameter based on your region        SQL Server Name will be passed from parent scope           - dbserver          Parameters set above               Azure Resource Manager (ARM) Template     https://github.com/Azure/arm-templates
